# Project Guidelines

## Code Style

- Keep changes small and consistent with the existing Astro codebase.
- Preserve the current French-first product copy and labels unless the task explicitly asks for another language.
- Reuse the existing structure in [src/pages/index.astro](../src/pages/index.astro), [src/pages/donjons/[slug].astro](../src/pages/donjons/[slug].astro), and [src/styles/global.css](../src/styles/global.css) instead of introducing new abstractions for simple UI changes.

## Architecture

- This is a static Astro site. Pages are generated from the `dungeons` content collection defined in [src/content/config.ts](../src/content/config.ts).
- Dungeon content lives in YAML files under [src/content/dungeons](../src/content/dungeons). Keep new fields aligned with the Zod schema before using them in pages.
- The homepage groups dungeons by `season` and sorts them by `order`.
- Dungeon detail pages are generated by `getStaticPaths()` in [src/pages/donjons/[slug].astro](../src/pages/donjons/[slug].astro).

## Build And Test

- Install dependencies with `npm install`.
- Start local development with `npm run dev`.
- Validate changes with `npm run build`.
- Preview the static output with `npm run preview`.
- There is no dedicated automated test suite in this repository, so use a successful build as the main validation step.

## Content Conventions

- Keep dungeon file names in kebab-case and make sure the file name matches the generated slug exactly.
- Preserve the existing content model: French `name`, English `englishName`, short route/bonus copy, and compact bullet points for `bosses` and `trash`.
- Keep season values consistent with the current site logic. The homepage currently relies on exact values such as `s1` and `Hors saison`.
- Important calls use emoji-prefixed labels such as `🔇`, `💊`, `⚠️`, and `✨`. Preserve that format because the UI derives chip styling from the text.

## Creating New Dungeon Documentation

**IMPORTANT**: Always consult the dungeon documentation workflow stored in repository memory at `/memories/repo/dungeon-documentation-workflow.md` when creating or editing dungeon files. This guide contains:
- Step-by-step process to add boss mechanics
- How to find official Wowhead FR spell names (never use custom translations)
- Standard YAML format and template
- Emoji usage guide (🛡️💊🗡️)
- Complete validation checklist
- Estimated timing per dungeon (~55 minutes)

For each boss ability:
1. Get spell ID from Method.gg dungeon guide
2. Verify official FR name on `https://www.wowhead.com/fr/spell=SPELL_ID`
3. Use format: `[Official Name](https://www.wowhead.com/fr/spell=ID/slug-name) : EMOJIS Description`
4. Run `npm run build` to validate

## Pitfalls

- When adding a new dungeon, update both the YAML entry and the manual image lookup tables in [src/pages/index.astro](../src/pages/index.astro) and [src/pages/donjons/[slug].astro](../src/pages/donjons/[slug].astro).
- Keep dungeon images aligned in both [src/assets](../src/assets) and [public/assets](../public/assets).
- Do not rely on dynamic runtime asset paths for dungeon images. This site imports images statically.
- If you change collection fields or season values, verify both the homepage filtering and the dungeon detail page still build correctly.
- **Never use custom spell name translations** — always use the official Wowhead FR translation instead (e.g., "Bond téméraire" not "Saut Impulsif")
